services.seoDescription: All driver training at DriveBuddies Zurich: Car (Cat. B) with manual or automatic, Motorcycle (A1/A), Trailer (BE) – flexible, personal, successful.
services.title: Choose your category
services.subtitle: Whether car, motorcycle or trailer – we guide you through the full training all the way to your licence.

auto.seoTitle: Car Training Cat. B – Manual & Automatic | DriveBuddies
auto.seoDescription: Car training Category B in Zurich: manual or automatic gearbox, individual lessons, VKU, exam preparation. Sign up with DriveBuddies today.
auto.title: Car – Manual or Automatic
auto.lead: Whether you want to master the manual gearbox or prefer an automatic – we tailor the training to your needs. All vehicles are modern and well maintained.
auto.manualText: The classic choice: a manual gives you full flexibility on all road types. We take the time to explain the technique clearly – even when it takes a little longer at first.
auto.automaticText: An automatic makes driving easier and is ideal when you want to focus fully on traffic. Please note: a licence issued on an automatic is valid for automatic vehicles only.
included: Individual lessons at your own pace
Grundkurs theory and practical (if not yet completed)
Exam preparation and accompaniment to the exam
auto.ctaWhatsappMessage: I am interested in car training (Cat. B) at DriveBuddies. Please get in touch.

motorrad.seoTitle: Motorcycle Training A1 & A – Grundkurs & Lessons | DriveBuddies
motorrad.seoDescription: Motorcycle training in Zurich: Grundkurs and lessons for A1 (125cc), A35kW and unrestricted A. Confident on two wheels with DriveBuddies.
motorrad.title: Motorcycle – A1 & A
motorrad.lead: From light motorcycle to full-power bike: we guide you through every stage of motorcycle training – from the first Grundkurs to the exam.
motorrad.a1Text: With A1 you may ride motorcycles up to 125 cc and 11 kW. You need the Grundkurs and a Lernfahrausweis Cat. A1. Minimum age: 16.
motorrad.aText: At 20 you can go directly to A (35 kW), at 24 to unrestricted A – or step up after 2 years on the previous level. The Grundkurs for the relevant level is mandatory.
motorrad.grundkursText: The mandatory Grundkurs covers theory and practical exercises on a closed course. We run Grundkurse for A1 and A in small groups with personal coaching.
motorrad.fahrstundenText: After the Grundkurs you ride on public roads with an experienced Fahrlehrer. The number of Fahrstunden depends on prior experience and progress.
included: Grundkurs A1 or A (theory + practical, mandatory)
motorrad.ctaWhatsappMessage: I am interested in motorcycle training (Cat. A/A1) at DriveBuddies. Please get in touch.

anhaenger.seoTitle: Trailer Training Cat. BE – Manoeuvring & Driving | DriveBuddies
anhaenger.seoDescription: Trailer training (Cat. BE) in Zurich: safe manoeuvring, cornering and braking with a trailer. Sign up with DriveBuddies.
anhaenger.title: Trailer – Cat. BE
anhaenger.lead: You have a Cat. B licence and want to tow a trailer? With trailer training you learn to manoeuvre safely, brake confidently and keep control even in tight situations.
anhaenger.requiresText: For Cat. BE training you need a valid Cat. B licence. The exam includes a practical drive with trailer and a manoeuvring exercise.
anhaenger.contentText: We practise coupling and uncoupling, reversing with a trailer, motorway driving, tight corners and correct braking with a loaded trailer.
included: Lessons with your own or a rented trailer
anhaenger.ctaWhatsappMessage: I am interested in trailer training (Cat. BE) at DriveBuddies. Please get in touch.

fuehrerschein.seoTitle: How to Get Your Swiss Driving Licence – Step-by-Step Guide
fuehrerschein.seoDescription: Complete guide to getting a Swiss driving licence (Führerausweis) Cat. B: Nothelfer, eye test, Lernfahrausweis, theory exam, VKU, driving test and WAB.
fuehrerschein.eyebrow: Your path to the Swiss licence
fuehrerschein.title: How to Get Your Swiss Driving Licence
fuehrerschein.lead: From the Nothelfer first-aid course to the definitive Führerausweis – here you'll find every step of the Swiss driver training explained clearly. DriveBuddies guides you through each phase.
fuehrerschein.timelineTitle: The 7 phases of Swiss driver training

steps.title: Nothelferkurs (First-Aid Course)
steps.text: Before you can apply for the Lernfahrausweis, you must complete a Nothelferkurs (approx. 10 hours). The certificate has no expiry date.

steps.title: Eye Test & Lernfahrausweis
steps.text: With your Nothelfer certificate and a current eye test, apply for the Lernfahrausweis at the cantonal Strassenverkehrsamt. Minimum age: 17.

steps.title: Theory Exam (Theorieprüfung)
steps.text: The theory exam consists of 70 computer-based questions. You need at least 57 correct. You can take it at any point during training.

steps.title: Driving Lessons (Fahrstunden)
steps.text: With your Lernfahrausweis you take lessons with a qualified Fahrlehrer. The number of lessons depends on your progress.

steps.title: VKU – Traffic Knowledge Course
steps.text: The VKU covers 2 half-days and is mandatory before the practical driving test. Topics: road safety, eco-driving, attitudes.

steps.title: Practical Driving Test
steps.text: After the VKU you sit the practical exam (approx. 45 min). If you pass, you receive the Probeführerausweis (3-year probationary period).

steps.title: 2-Phasen-Ausbildung (WAB)
steps.text: Within the first 12 months of your probationary period you must complete the mandatory WAB course. After 3 years without serious offences you receive the definitive Führerausweis.

faq.title: Questions about the Swiss driving licence

items.q: How long does the Swiss driver training take?
items.a: The duration varies individually. From registration to the exam it typically takes 6–12 months, depending on learning progress, appointment availability and the time needed for mandatory steps such as the Nothelfer course and VKU.

items.q: How much does a Swiss driving licence cost?
items.a: The total costs include: Nothelfer course (approx. CHF 100–120), Lernfahrausweis (approx. CHF 60), theory exam (approx. CHF 50–80), driving lessons (varies), VKU (approx. CHF 220–280), exam fee (approx. CHF 160–250) and WAB course (approx. CHF 500–600). Expect to spend CHF 2,000–4,000 in total.

items.q: At what age can I start learning to drive?
items.a: You can apply for the Lernfahrausweis from age 17. The practical driving test may not be taken before age 18.

items.q: How many driving lessons are mandatory?
items.a: Switzerland has no minimum number of lessons. 20–30 hours is typical. After a trial lesson your Fahrlehrer can give you a realistic estimate.

fuehrerschein.ctaText: DriveBuddies guides you through every step – from the Nothelfer course to your Führerausweis.
fuehrerschein.ctaWhatsappMessage: I'd like to start my driver training. Please get in touch.

nothelferkurs.seoTitle: Nothelferkurs Zurich – Mandatory Before the Lernfahrausweis | DriveBuddies
nothelferkurs.seoDescription: Nothelferkurs in Zurich: why it's mandatory, what it costs and where to take it. Information from DriveBuddies driving school.
nothelferkurs.breadcrumbHub: How to get your Swiss licence
nothelferkurs.title: Nothelferkurs – Mandatory Before the Lernfahrausweis
nothelferkurs.lead: Before you apply for the Lernfahrausweis in Switzerland you must complete a Nothelferkurs (first-aid course). It lasts approximately 10 hours and prepares you for first-aid situations on the road.
nothelferkurs.whatText: The Nothelferkurs (also called Erste-Hilfe-Kurs) is required by Swiss law and must be completed before the Lernfahrausweis is issued. You learn how to respond correctly at accident scenes – from securing the scene to performing CPR.
contentItems: Securing the accident scene and warning other road users

facts.label: Providers
facts.value: TCS, Samariter, Red Cross, REGA etc.

nothelferkurs.whereText: The course is offered by recognised organisations such as the TCS (Touring Club Switzerland), Samaritan associations and the Swiss Red Cross. Regular courses are available in Zurich, Thalwil and surrounding areas.
nothelferkurs.nextText: After the Nothelferkurs: complete the eye test and apply for the Lernfahrausweis at the Strassenverkehrsamt.

items.q: Must I take the Nothelferkurs before the Lernfahrausweis?
items.a: Yes. The Nothelferkurs must be completed before the Lernfahrausweis is issued. The Strassenverkehrsamt will not issue the learner permit without a Nothelfer certificate.

items.q: How long is the Nothelferkurs certificate valid?
items.a: The Nothelfer certificate has no expiry date for the purposes of the Lernfahrausweis application. It remains valid indefinitely.

items.q: Can I take the Nothelferkurs online?
items.a: No. The Nothelferkurs must be completed in person because practical exercises (e.g. CPR) can only be practised in a group setting.

items.q: Can DriveBuddies recommend a Nothelferkurs?
items.a: Yes. Contact us – we are happy to point you to a suitable course provider in Zurich or Thalwil.

nothelferkurs.ctaText: We help you find the right course and guide you through the full driver training journey.
nothelferkurs.ctaWhatsappMessage: I have questions about the Nothelferkurs and getting started with driver training. Please get in touch.

theoriepruefung.seoTitle: Swiss Theory Exam (Theorieprüfung) – How It Works | DriveBuddies
theoriepruefung.seoDescription: The Swiss theory exam: 70 questions, 57 correct to pass. Everything about the process, costs, preparation tips and common mistakes.
theoriepruefung.breadcrumbHub: How to get your Swiss licence
theoriepruefung.title: Swiss Theory Exam – How to Prepare and Pass
theoriepruefung.lead: The Theorieprüfung is a mandatory step towards your driving licence. You answer 70 computer-based questions – at least 57 must be correct.
ablaufItems: Register at the cantonal Strassenverkehrsamt or an authorised test centre
Computer-based exam: 70 questions on traffic rules, hazard perception and vehicle technology
Pass mark: at least 57 out of 70 correct (≥ 81.4 %)
If you fail: you may retake the exam after 7 days

facts.label: Language
facts.value: DE, FR, IT, EN and others

tipsItems: Study with an official learning app (e.g. theorie24 or TCS)
Practise regularly – 20 min daily beats a 3-hour session once a week
Focus on questions you frequently get wrong
Take mock exams until you consistently score above 90 %
Pay attention to hazard perception – these questions cost many candidates points

items.q: When can I take the theory exam?
items.a: You can take the theory exam once you have received the Lernfahrausweis. It can also be taken earlier if you have reached the minimum age of 17.

items.q: How long is the theory exam valid?
items.a: A passed theory exam is valid for 2 years. The practical driving test must be taken within this period.

items.q: In which language can I take the theory exam?
items.a: The theory exam is available in many languages including German, French, Italian, English, Albanian, Bosnian, Croatian, Portuguese, Serbian, Spanish and others.

items.q: What happens if I fail the theory exam?
items.a: You can retake the exam after 7 days. After failing twice, the waiting period between attempts increases.

theoriepruefung.nextText: After the theory exam: take driving lessons and complete the VKU.
theoriepruefung.ctaText: Our experienced instructors prepare you for both the theory exam and the practical driving test.
theoriepruefung.ctaWhatsappMessage: I'd like to prepare for the theory exam. Please get in touch.

vku.seoTitle: VKU Zurich – Traffic Knowledge Course Before the Driving Test | DriveBuddies
vku.seoDescription: VKU in Zurich: the Verkehrskundeunterricht is mandatory before the practical driving exam. 2 half-days, topics, costs and registration – everything explained.
vku.breadcrumbHub: How to get your Swiss licence
vku.title: VKU – Traffic Knowledge Course in Zurich
vku.lead: The VKU (Verkehrskundeunterricht) is a legally required course that you must complete before the practical driving test. It lasts 2 half-days and covers road safety and eco-driving.
vku.whatText: The Verkehrskundeunterricht (VKU) complements the practical driving training. Unlike Fahrstunden, the focus here is not on controlling the car but on knowledge, attitudes and road competence. The course is held in groups.
contentItems: Risk awareness and hazard perception in road traffic
Effects of alcohol, drugs and medication on driving ability
Behaviour at accidents and basic legal framework
vku.atDbText: As a qualified driving school DriveBuddies runs VKU courses in Zurich. Courses are held in small groups and can be attended in German or English.

items.q: When do I have to complete the VKU?
items.a: The VKU must be completed before the practical driving test. It must be finished no more than 12 months before the exam and at least 3 months after receiving the Lernfahrausweis.

items.q: Can I take the VKU in English?
items.a: Yes. At DriveBuddies we offer the VKU in German and English – just let us know.

items.q: How do I register for the VKU?
items.a: You can register directly with DriveBuddies. Contact us by phone, WhatsApp or via our contact form.

items.q: Can I take the VKU at a different driving school from my lessons?
items.a: Yes. The VKU does not have to be taken at your regular driving school. You can complete it at any recognised driving school.

vku.nextText: After the VKU: register for the practical driving test.
vku.ctaText: We run VKU courses in German and English. Sign up now.
vku.ctaWhatsappMessage: I'd like to book the VKU (Verkehrskundeunterricht) at DriveBuddies. Please get in touch.

zweiPhasen.seoTitle: 2-Phasen-Ausbildung & WAB Zurich – How It Works | DriveBuddies
zweiPhasen.seoDescription: The 2-Phasen-Ausbildung (WAB) is mandatory after the driving test. Everything about the WAB course, deadlines, costs and the path to the definitive Führerausweis.
zweiPhasen.breadcrumbHub: How to get your Swiss licence
zweiPhasen.title: 2-Phasen-Ausbildung & WAB – The Path to Your Definitive Führerausweis
zweiPhasen.lead: You passed the driving test – congratulations! Now begins the 3-year probationary period with the Probeführerausweis. Within the first 12 months you must complete the mandatory WAB course (Weiterausbildungsphase).
zweiPhasen.probeText: After passing the practical test you receive a Probeführerausweis (probationary licence), valid for 3 years. Special rules apply during this period:
probeItems: Alcohol while driving: 0.0 ‰ (instead of 0.5 ‰ for experienced drivers)
Serious traffic offences can lead to revocation of the Probeführerausweis
Within 12 months of passing: WAB course mandatory
After 3 years without serious offences: automatic conversion to definitive Führerausweis
zweiPhasen.wabText: The Weiterausbildungsphase (WAB) consists of a course at a recognised road safety training centre. The course spans 2 days and combines theoretical elements with practical exercises on the training grounds.
wabContentItems: Vehicle dynamics training on the safety training ground
Handling aquaplaning, evasive manoeuvres and emergency braking
Theoretical deepening on safety and attitudes
Drive on public roads with instructor feedback

facts.label: Deadline
facts.value: within 12 months of passing the test

items.q: What happens if I don't complete the WAB course on time?
items.a: If you don't complete the WAB course within 12 months of passing the test, the Probeführerausweis becomes invalid. You must then retake the practical driving test.

items.q: Where can I take the WAB course?
items.a: The WAB course must be completed at a recognised road safety training centre, e.g. TCS (Verkehrssicherheitszentrum), BUSA or other bfu-approved centres in Switzerland.

items.q: How much does the WAB cost?
items.a: Costs vary by provider and generally range from CHF 500 to CHF 650 for the 2 course days.

items.q: What counts as a serious offence during the probationary period?
items.a: Serious offences include: driving under the influence of alcohol (from 0.0 ‰ for new drivers), significantly exceeding the speed limit, reckless overtaking or jumping a red light. A serious offence extends the probationary period by 1 year.

zweiPhasen.ctaText: DriveBuddies guides you from your first lesson to the definitive Führerausweis.
zweiPhasen.ctaWhatsappMessage: I have questions about the 2-Phasen-Ausbildung (WAB). Please get in touch.

hero.title: Reach your goal safely – with DriveBuddies
hero.subtitle: Modern vehicles, flexible times and experienced Fahrlehrer. We guide you all the way from the Lernfahrausweis to the Führerausweis.

usp.title: Why DriveBuddies?
usp.subtitle: Three reasons you'll get your licence faster and more relaxed with us.

items.title: Modern vehicles
items.text: Near-new cars with the latest driver-assistance systems – safe, clean and pleasant to drive.

items.title: Flexible times
items.text: Evenings, weekends or at short notice: we schedule your lessons around your daily life.

items.title: Experienced instructors
items.text: Patient, multilingual Fahrlehrer with years of experience and a high pass rate.

services.title: Our training programmes
services.subtitle: From your first Grundkurs to the exam – pick your category.

items.title: Car – Cat. B
items.text: Complete car training including Grundkurs, VKU and exam preparation.

items.title: Motorcycle – Cat. A
items.text: Grundkurse and lessons for A1, A35kW and unrestricted A – confident on two wheels.

items.title: Trailer – Cat. BE
items.text: Training for driving with a trailer and heavier combinations – manoeuvre with ease.

journey.title: How the Swiss driver training works
journey.intro: We guide you through every stage – from the first mandatory courses to the second training phase.

steps.title: Lernfahrausweis
steps.text: Complete the Nothelfer course and eye test, then apply for the Lernfahrausweis at the Strassenverkehrsamt.

steps.title: Führerausweis
steps.text: Attend the VKU, take driving lessons and pass the practical Prüfung.

steps.title: 2-Phasen-Ausbildung
steps.text: Complete the mandatory second training phase (WAB) within your probationary period.

instructor.name: Your DriveBuddies team
instructor.text: We believe in personal, patient training as equals. With a modern car, clear communication and a pace that suits you, we'll get you safely through the exam – and beyond.

faq.title: Answers to the most common questions

items.q: What do I need to start driving?
items.a: You'll need the Nothelfer course, a passed eye test and the Lernfahrausweis from the Strassenverkehrsamt. After that you can start your lessons.

items.q: How many driving lessons do I need?
items.a: It's individual. After a first trial lesson we estimate together how many lessons you'll need before the exam.

items.q: Do you offer lessons in English?
items.a: Yes. Our Fahrlehrer teach in German and English – so you feel comfortable from the very start.

finalCta.title: Ready for your first lesson?
finalCta.text: Get in touch today and start your driver training with DriveBuddies – flexible, personal and successful.

preise.seoTitle: Driving School Prices Zurich – Lessons & Courses | DriveBuddies
preise.seoDescription: Transparent pricing at DriveBuddies driving school Zurich: car & motorcycle lessons, Grundkurs, trailer package, VKU and bundle deals. All prices in CHF.
preise.title: Prices & Packages
preise.lead: All prices at a glance – no hidden costs. Lessons, Grundkurs, packages and the insurance note are all listed clearly below.
preise.lessonRatesNote: Price per lesson (50 minutes) including vehicle use.
preise.grundkursNote: Mandatory Grundkurs – theory and practical exercises on the training ground.
preise.trailerNote: Exam-ready in one package: lessons, manoeuvring course and exam prep.
preise.bundleNote: Book multiple lessons upfront and save.
preise.vkuNote: Mandatory course before the driving test – 2 half-days at DriveBuddies.
preise.versicherungText: Each driving lesson includes an insurance surcharge of CHF {amount}. This covers comprehensive vehicle insurance and liability insurance during the lesson.
preise.ctaText: Questions about pricing or ready to book? Get in touch – we respond quickly.
preise.ctaWhatsappMessage: I'm interested in driver training at DriveBuddies and would like to make an appointment.
preise.disclaimer: All prices in CHF, VAT included. Prices subject to change. Fees charged by the Strassenverkehrsamt (exam, Lernfahrausweis etc.) are not included.
